01_initial_request.input_data: protein samples from patient derived xenograft (PDX) models and human core needle biopsies
01_initial_request.user_intent: To develop an optimized kinase inhibitor pulldown (KiP) assay for accurate quantification of the kinome in clinical biopsy samples with low protein yields
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Accurate quantification of a 100-kinase panel with improved quantitative fidelity in microgram-level protein samples

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Kinase inhibitors can be used as an affinity matrix for kinase enrichment. [Gap] Commercial ECH Sepharose 4B was discontinued, requiring a custom synthesis protocol for kinobeads. [Decision] To achieve robust kinase capture, chose to use CNBr-activated Sepharose 4B and EDC chemistry to conjugate 9 kinase inhibitors to beads, expecting to obtain a functional 9KiP reagent.

parameters.inhibitors: Palbociclib, Crizotinib, GSK690693, AZD4547, CZC-8004, Modified Afatinib, FRAX597, Abemaciclib, Axitinib

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Successfully synthesized 9KiP reagent stored in 20% ethanol at 4°C.

references.relevance: Protocol for conjugation of drugs to beads.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Clinical biopsy samples yield low protein amounts (microgram-level). [Gap] Existing kinobead protocols require milligram quantities. [Decision] To achieve enrichment in low-input samples, chose to use 9KiP beads to perform kinase inhibitor pulldown (KiP) on 20-200 µg of lysate, expecting to obtain enriched kinase peptides.

parameters.wash_buffer: 50 mM HEPES, 600 mM NaCl, 0.5% Triton X-100

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Kinases bound to beads, unbound proteins removed in supernatant.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Enriched proteins need to be digested for MS analysis. [Gap] Detergents used in lysis interfere with MS. [Decision] To prepare samples for MS, chose to use Trypsin for on-bead digestion and HiPPR kit for detergent removal, expecting to obtain clean peptide mixtures.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Digested peptide mixture dried by speed-vac prior to MS analysis.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Discovery of kinase targets requires broad scanning. [Gap] Optimal peptides for PRM need to be identified. [Decision] To identify kinase peptides, chose to use Orbitrap Fusion Lumos to perform DDA acquisition, expecting to obtain a list of identifiable kinase peptides.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DDA spectra generated for database searching.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw MS data needs to be converted to peptide identifications. [Gap] Peptide spectrum matches need validation. [Decision] To process DDA data, chose to use Proteome Discoverer with Mascot engine, expecting to obtain validated PSMs at 5% FDR.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: PSM validation performed and rolled up to gene products.

references.relevance: Data processing workflow and validation tools.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PRM requires specific peptide targets. [Gap] Not all identified peptides are suitable for quantification. [Decision] To select optimal PRM targets, chose to use Skyline and manual evaluation to filter peptides based on score and peak shape, expecting to obtain a panel of 2-4 peptides per kinase.

parameters.selection_criteria: Mascot Ion Score > 20, < 10% PTM, no missed cleavage, gene-specific

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 100-kinase panel peptides selected for PRM assay.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Accurate quantification requires targeted acquisition. [Gap] DDA is stochastic for low abundance kinases. [Decision] To improve quantification, chose to use Orbitrap Fusion Lumos to perform PRM acquisition, expecting to obtain precise intensity measurements for targeted peptides.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: PRM spectra collected for targeted kinases.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PRM data needs quantification. [Gap] Peak areas need to be integrated accurately. [Decision] To quantify kinases, chose to use Skyline to calculate peptide area from product ions, expecting to obtain normalized protein levels.

parameters.ions_used: Up to six strongest product ions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein levels quantified with improved fidelity for low abundance kinases.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The assay needs validation in biological models. [Gap] Subtyping capability in PDX models is unproven. [Decision] To validate subtyping, chose to use Hierarchical Clustering on KiP-PRM data from 16 PDX models, expecting to observe separation by basal and luminal subtypes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Tumors separated into groups corresponding to basal and luminal PAM50 subtypes.

references.relevance: PDX model characterization and PAM50 subtyping.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Clinical utility requires testing on patient samples. [Gap] Performance on core needle biopsies is unknown. [Decision] To validate clinical applicability, chose to use K-means Clustering on KiP-PRM data from 37 patient samples, expecting to observe clustering by Luminal and HER2 subtypes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Samples yielded 3 subgroups: one Luminal and two HER2 groups, consistent with subtypes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Clinical implementation requires high throughput and absolute quantification. [Gap] Label-free PRM lacks internal standards. [Decision] To define a prototype clinical method, chose to use Orbitrap Exploris 480 with Evosep One to perform IS-PRM (SureQuant), expecting to obtain absolute quantification with heavy peptides.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: IS-PRM data acquired with triggered acquisition for endogenous peptides.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Precision of the clinical prototype needs assessment. [Gap] Variability of IS-PRM measurements is unknown. [Decision] To assess precision, chose to use Skyline to analyze IS-PRM replicates, expecting to obtain coefficient of variance (CV) metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Average CV of 9.28% achieved, clustering recapitulated PRM data.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: KiP-PRM and IS-PRM subtyping analysis with heavy peptide standards

kinase_panel_size.interpretation: Target panel size achieved for quantification.

sample_input.interpretation: Assay optimized for microgram-level protein samples typical of biopsies.

quantitative_precision.interpretation: Average coefficient of variance for 80 peptides in IS-PRM assay.

subtyping_accuracy.interpretation: PDX and patient samples clustered correctly by intrinsic subtype (Luminal/HER2/Basal).

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Achieved: The study successfully developed an optimized KiP assay for accurate quantification of a 100-kinase panel in microgram-level protein samples, validated by subtyping PDX and clinical biopsy samples with high quantitative fidelity.
